name: Kyrenia Shipwreck
description: Well-preserved Greek merchant vessel carrying wine amphorae and millstones, one of the best-preserved ancient wrecks.

name: Mahdia Shipwreck
description: Greek vessel carrying looted art treasures including bronze and marble sculptures from Athens to Italy.

name: Antikythera Wreck
description: Famous wreck yielding the Antikythera mechanism, bronze and marble statues, luxury glassware, and amphorae.

name: Madrague de Giens
description: Large Roman freighter carrying 6,000-8,000 Dressel 1B wine amphorae from Italy to Gaul.

name: Grand Congloue A
description: Early Republican-era wreck near Marseille carrying Greek and Italic wine amphorae.

name: Grand Congloue B
description: Late Republican wreck carrying Dressel 1A wine amphorae and Campanian pottery.

name: Cap Dramont A
description: Roman merchant vessel carrying Dressel 1 wine amphorae along the southern French coast.

name: Titan Wreck
description: Roman cargo vessel carrying wine amphorae off the Iles d'Hyeres, southern France.

name: Spargi Wreck
description: Roman merchant ship wrecked off Sardinia carrying Dressel 1 wine amphorae and fine pottery.

name: Albenga Wreck
description: Large Roman cargo ship carrying over 10,000 wine amphorae, one of the largest ancient cargoes found.

name: Uluburun Successor
description: Hellenistic-era trading vessel near the Lycian coast carrying amphorae and bronze implements.

name: Serce Limani Hellenistic
description: Hellenistic merchant ship near Rhodes carrying Rhodian and Knidian transport amphorae.

name: Caesarea Maritima A
description: Construction supply vessel wrecked during the building of Herod's harbor, carrying pozzolana and stone blocks.

name: Nemi Ship A
description: Elaborate pleasure barge of Emperor Caligula from Lake Nemi, featuring lead pipes and marble floors.

name: Cavaliere Wreck
description: Roman merchant vessel carrying Italian wine amphorae along the Provencal coast.

name: Comacchio Wreck
description: Augustan-era coastal vessel in the Po Delta carrying lead ingots, wine amphorae, and fine pottery.

name: La Chretienne A
description: Mid-Republican wreck carrying Greco-Italic amphorae on the Italy-to-Gaul route.

name: Cabrera III
description: Early Punic-era wreck near the Balearic Islands carrying North African amphorae.

name: Skerki Bank A
description: Deep-water wreck on the Africa-to-Italy shipping lane in the Strait of Sicily.

name: Skerki Bank B
description: Imperial-era deep-water wreck carrying African and Spanish amphorae across the central Mediterranean.

name: Porticello Wreck
description: Hellenistic wreck in the Strait of Messina carrying bronze statuary and ceramic cargo.

name: Capistello Wreck
description: Hellenistic trader off the Aeolian Islands carrying Greco-Italic wine amphorae.

name: Punta Scaletta
description: Roman vessel wrecked off Giannutri island carrying Greco-Italic amphorae.

name: Bou Ferrer Wreck
description: Large Roman merchantman off Villajoyosa, Spain carrying Dressel 7-11 fish sauce amphorae and lead ingots.

name: Yassiada Byzantine
description: Late Roman cargo ship near Bodrum carrying late amphorae during the twilight of Roman trade.

name: Plemmirio B
description: Wreck near Syracuse dating to the Second Punic War period, carrying Greek wine amphorae.

name: Marsala Punic Ship
description: Punic warship likely sunk during the Battle of the Aegates Islands in the First Punic War.

name: Egadi Islands Ram Site
description: Battle of the Aegates Islands debris field with bronze warship rams from the climax of the First Punic War.

name: Dramont E
description: One of the latest Roman trade vessels found on the Provencal coast, carrying Eastern Mediterranean amphorae.

name: Jeaune Garde B
description: Mid-Republican vessel near Toulon carrying Italian wine for the Gallic market.

name: Plane I
description: Early Republican wreck near the Calanques of Marseille carrying Greco-Italic amphorae.

name: Agde A
description: Hellenistic coastal wreck near Agde carrying Greek amphorae to the western Mediterranean.

name: Port-Vendres II
description: Claudian-era vessel carrying tin and copper ingots, fish sauce amphorae, and fine pottery from Spain to Italy.

name: Cap Bear III
description: Republican cargo ship near the French-Spanish border carrying Italian wine amphorae.

name: Cala Cativa
description: Roman merchant vessel wrecked on the Catalan coast carrying Greco-Italic and Punic amphorae.

name: Culip IV
description: Flavian-era wreck at Cap de Creus carrying Dressel 20 olive oil amphorae from Baetica.

name: Cabrera V
description: Late Republican wreck in the Balearics carrying Spanish and Italian wine amphorae.

name: Porto Cristo
description: Early Imperial wreck off Mallorca carrying Baetican fish sauce amphorae.

name: Sud-Lavezzi II
description: Augustan-era wreck in the Strait of Bonifacio carrying marble columns and amphorae.

name: Lavezzi I
description: Republican wreck in the treacherous Strait of Bonifacio carrying Dressel 1 wine amphorae.

name: Pisa Ship A
description: Roman vessel found in the silted harbor of Pisa carrying wine and grain cargo.

name: Pisa Ship C
description: Imperial-era merchant ship from the Pisa harbor complex carrying Mediterranean trade goods.

name: Fiumicino I
description: Grain transport vessel at Portus, the harbor of Rome, part of the annona supply system.

name: Fiumicino III
description: Trajanic grain freighter at Portus, built for the Egyptian grain run.

name: Laurons II
description: Late Antonine coastal vessel near Marseille carrying African amphorae.

name: Dor D
description: Flavian-era vessel wrecked near the ancient harbor of Dor on the Levantine coast.

name: Ognina Wreck
description: Republican-era vessel off southeastern Sicily carrying Greek and Punic transport amphorae.

name: Punta Scifo D
description: Marble carrier off Crotone transporting columns and architectural elements from the eastern Mediterranean.

name: Torre Sgarrata
description: Late Antonine marble ship wrecked off Taranto carrying unfinished sarcophagi from Proconnesus.

name: San Pietro Wreck
description: Republican vessel off southwestern Sardinia carrying lead and copper ingots from Iberian mines.

name: Riace Wreck
description: Wreck off Calabria famous for yielding the Riace bronzes, large Greek warrior statues.

name: Melilla Wreck
description: Republican-era cargo ship off the North African coast near Melilla carrying Italian wine.

name: Chretienne C
description: Mid-Republican vessel off Saint-Raphael carrying Dressel 1A wine amphorae to Gaul.

name: Barthelemy B
description: Late Republican wreck near Frejus carrying wine and olive oil amphorae.

name: Tradeliere Wreck
description: Late Republican vessel on the Riviera coast carrying Campanian wine amphorae.

name: Dramont D
description: Early Imperial wreck carrying roof tiles and ceramic building materials from Italy to Gaul.

name: Planier III
description: Late Republican vessel near Marseille carrying Dressel 1B and Dressel 12 amphorae.

name: Ile de la Giraglia
description: Augustan vessel wrecked at the northern tip of Corsica carrying Dressel 2-4 wine amphorae.

name: La Luque B

description: Mid-Republican wreck off southern Sicily carrying Greek and Italian wine amphorae.

name: Marzamemi Church Wreck

name: Thonis-Heracleion A

name: Cala Culip I

name: Torre Chianca

name: Agay A
description: Late Republican vessel off Agay on the Var coast carrying wine to Gaul.

description: Antonine merchant vessel off the southern coast of Cyprus carrying mixed cargo.

description: Flavian vessel in the Gulf of Iskenderun near ancient Issus carrying Syrian goods.

description: Mid-Republican wreck near Gallinaria off the Ligurian coast carrying wine to Gaul.

description: Severan-era vessel near ancient Hadrumetum carrying Byzacenian olive oil for export.

name: Crotone Offshore
description: Hellenistic wreck off ancient Kroton carrying Greek wine and pottery from Magna Graecia.

name: Kas Wreck B
description: Trajanic trading vessel off the Lycian coast carrying Cilician and Aegean amphorae.

name: Melos Channel
description: Republican-era vessel in the Cyclades carrying mixed Aegean commercial cargo.

name: Delos Harbor
description: Late Republican wreck near the great trading emporium of Delos carrying diverse Mediterranean goods.

name: Ostia Offshore A
description: Flavian grain ship in the approaches to Ostia, Rome's primary port.

name: Ostia Offshore B
description: Antonine merchant vessel at Ostia carrying African olive oil and Spanish fish sauce.

name: Ventimiglia Wreck
description: Late Republican vessel off the Italian-French border carrying wine on the coastal route.

name: Marettimo Wreck
description: Mid-Republican wreck near the Egadi Islands on the busy Africa-to-Sicily corridor.

name: Taormina Offshore
description: Tiberian vessel off the northeast coast of Sicily near Tauromenium.

name: Amalfi Coast Wreck
description: Flavian-era merchant ship off the Amalfi coast carrying Campanian wine exports.

name: Abbeville
description: "Celtic" or "continental" boat (cf. Zwammerdam?) found in the Somme river

name: Acquaviva
description: status as wreck, rather than jettison, unclear

name: Acque Chiare
description: 300-400 m off shore; remains of wreck scattered over 50 m; looted

name: Agay
description: skeleton of man with sword and leather sheath; dating from C10 Muslim ceramic; ship built of small timbers, skeleton first; 1 (=ship's boat?) and possibly 2 ships nearby; possible naval battle; some of the objects point to Alicante area, Murcia

name: Agde 3
description: pieces of wood, 500 m from coast

name: Akandia 1
description: 550 m NE of bay; previously looted

name: Alblasserdam
description: dugout found close to Roman-period buildings

name: Ardenza
description: heavily looted; many amphoras; pieces of the ship present

name: Arles-Rhône
description: in the river bed of the Rhone; possibly a wreck

name: Avenches 2
description: caulked Roman boat, dendrodated, probably abandoned in C2

name: Ayia Galini
description: lead from hull sheathing, 200 m from shore

name: Bacoli 1
description: frame and planking of ship's hull; several 1000 amphoras

name: Bagaud 1
description: The array of amphoras probably indicates another, late Roman wreck; for lead possibly from this wreck, see Parker 84

name: Baie de l'Amitié
description: remains of the hull; use of plane (platanus) wood and pollen in hull pitch show ship was built and fitted in eastern Mediterranean

name: Batiguier, Le
description: burned  hull, 3 human skeletons, Saracen ship; dated by style on decorative objects; skeleton first construction; flat-bottomed, similar to Agay and Serçe Liman

name: Ben-Afelí
description: remains of ship include small bronze cylinders of pump, lead sheathing, rolls of lead for patching, copper nails, tapering lead pipe, bronze sheave bearing, wooden pole; 300-1200 m from shore

name: Benicarlo
description: remains of lead sheathing, lead anchor stocks

name: Bevaix
description: boat and steering oar, timbers dated by 14C to 90+-60, by dendro: 182; flat-bottomed; mast; capacity of 7-10 tons

name: Bozburun
description: Paleobotanical analysis of amphora contents available. Over 1000 amphoras in cargo, majority for wine. Hull dated by dendro.

name: Brindisi
description: keel and planking of large medieval ship

name: Bruges
description: mast-step, mast, steering oar, planking, C14 180+-80; similar to Blackfriars boat

name: Cabrera 5
description: pieces of  ship, three pieces of lead drainpipes

name: Cala Cativa
description: surviving hull; 2 km east of Port de la Selva

name: Calanque de l'Âne
description: coin of Domitian in the mast-step

name: Calvi
description: Calvi 1 dated to end of 16th C.: Villié 1989

name: Cap del Vol
description: mast-step, sternpost, planking, lead sheets, hull

name: Cavallo 1
description: preserved hull, keel, some strakes, some frames

name: Conillera
description: extensive remains of ship; some of cargo came from Lusitania

name: Corte Cavanella
description: flat-bottomed boat in a cavana, a Venetian boat shelter. Left in situ.

name: Courreaux-de-Groix (France)
description: No direct evidence. Only one amphora.

name: Cudrefin
description: dugout, resembles Bronze Age boat, 14C end of Iron age or early Roman period

name: Femmina Morta
description: keep, frames, planking, and lead sheathing

name: Fiumicino 2
description: mast-step, yard, shaft of a steering oar, 14C: late 2nd C.

name: Flavigny-sur-Moselle (France)
description: dugout once part of raft, C14: 540 AD +/-80

name: Garoupe 1, La
description: Fiori says wreck occurred in 2nd half of C1 AD

name: Grado
description: lead-sheathing and frames, mortice and tenon, patched; pine, elm, rigging, part of sail; some oil amphoras recycled; at least 600 amphoras, total cargo weight 23-25 tons. Ship had been repaired more than once, and was probably fairly old (Beltrame and Gaddi 2007, 144; 146).

name: Heliopolis 1
description: keel, 200 m from shore; coin dated 300-310, one 5th C. amphora, 3rd C. wreck (according to Hayes) based on common wares, Joncheray dates to 4th C. like Pointe de la Luque.

name: Herculaneum
description: boat carbonized by volcanic mud, 500 m from modern coast

name: Iskandil Burnu 1
description: 2000+ globular amphoras. Main cargo appears to have come from Palestine.

name: Jezirat Fara'un (Sinai)
description: Probably anchorage debris of Byzantine  amphoras. Possibility of wreck deep under sand.

name: Ladispoli 1
description: well-preserved hull; McCann et al say 25-100?AD

name: Lara (Cyprus)
description: timbers with nails and lead sheathing

name: Laurons 1, Les
description: preserved ship, 46 m from shore - Late Roman vessel repaired numerous times

name: Mainz 2
description: single-banked galley, wood felled 376, repaired 385 and 394

name: Marritza
description: lead sheathing, large pulley, wooden beam of mast, roof tiles

name: Marsala 1
description: well-preserved Arab ship, 40 m from beach

name: 'Mediterranean'
description: No precise location info provided. W Mediterranean hypothesized

name: Miladou, Le
description: Miladou dated end C2 or first half of C1 BC, detailed analysis Dumontier and Joncheray 1991; ?dating based on coin of Constantine

name: Ognina 1
description: mosaic floor, columns with capitals flanking doorway

name: Palazzolo di Strella
description: central part of large Roman boat

name: Patresi (Italy)
description: unsure where and if there is a wreck.  Samples from C7 BC to C4 AD.  One Spanish C1 AD wreck thought to be here at 50m depth

name: Pedagne, Le (Italy)
description: At the mouth of the port of Brindisi. It's likely that there is more evidence. Area was militarized until the late 90s, so no excavations possible until then.

name: Pelosa 1
description: hull planking, deadeyes? A Roman and a medieval wreck seem to be on top of one another on this site of Pelosa; it is difficult to assign finds to one or the other.

name: Plane 3
description: partially-preserved hull; ceramic related to 10th C. Muslim production

name: Planier 1
description: Stamped ceramics date site to 1-25 and probably 1-10AD; 2 wood statues

name: Plemmirio 2
description: roof-tiles, bricks, stone blocks, pottery vaulting tube, lead sheathing and iron nail concretions of hull

name: Punta Ala
description: 3/4 hull, foremast step, foot of foremast

name: Roquetas del Mar (Spain)
description: Mixture of amphoras. Possibility of wreck.

name: Saint Peter Port
description: dating based on dendro (oak) as well as coins minted ca 260-80

name: Sardinia: other sites
description: P1039a Symbolic coordinates. No precise location info provided. W Mediterranean hypothesized

name: Serçe Limani Zone
description: hull remains, nails, caulking, 70 m off shore

name: Sile
description: metal objects and timber from hull

name: Tarragona (Spain)
description: The record is only for a an amphora brought back by a fishing vessel. Coordinates purely symbolic

name: Tel Kara (Israel)
description: Unpublished. Coordinates close to Parker's indication of Kefar Gallim, near location of the site.

name: Terrasini 1
description: wood fragments, copper nails, lead sheathing, tiles

name: Varna
description: amphoras now in Varna Archaeological Museum; from Black Sea? wine?

name: Woerden 1
description: river boat, frames, planks, mast-step, fireplace, dendrodated: 169 AD; ceramic a bit later; sandals C3 early, and indicated at least 3 adult crew members; many repairs. Presumably grain transport for Roman fort on site.

name: Velsen 1
description: Dating based on 14C. Oak paddled vessel of Utrecht 1 type in medieval context.

name: Cavo (Italy)
description: coins: dupondius of Antoninus Pius, bronze medallion of Marcus Aurelius.

name: Alonessos 2
description: only surveyed by sonar and preliminary dives. 200m N of Alonissos wreck.  This wreck has not been assigned a precise id in the publication.

name: Årby
description: No dating information provided in database. Pine, oak, oared, Viking-age working boat later used as a grave.

name: Äskekärr 1
description: No dating info in database. Oak, sailed, cargo Nordic ship.

name: Avdimou bay
description: Survey. Dating based on amphoras and anchors.  The survey mentions this site in passing.  See IJNA articles for  more details.

name: Baie de Conca
description: area used by dive clubs, so little found

name: Barland's farm
description: Dating based on oak dendrochronology. Cargo vessel. Romano-Celtic context.

name: Björke
description: Dating based on one 14C sample with a 70 year margin.  Paddled? lime, pine, spruce, juniper, Nordic-type, working boat.

name: Calanque de l'Âne 1
description: dated from ceramic, glass; bronze coin of Domitian for terminus post quem; S of Pointe de Pomègues; well preserved hull and deck

name: Camerat 1
description: dating based on decoration on tiles

name: Cape Zevgari, Cyprus
description: Survey discovery. Likely represents cargo of a small coastal trader

name: Casnewydd
description: Remains of a man under the ship. Scuttled and stripped out hull, edge-fastened  by iron nails, 10m mast step.

name: Charbrowo 1
description: Dating based on dendro (15 samples with interval 1112-1190) and 14C (2 samples with interval 920-930).  All dating done on planks.  Discovered in 1896. Slavonic context. Oak, pine and juniper vessel, sailed/oared.

name: Czarnowsko 1
description: Dating based on 14C (3 samples with interval 910-1010) and dendro (18 samples from planks with interval 1138-1174).   Pine, oak oared/sailed, oared/sailed Slavonic working boat  in Slavonic context. Discovered 1957.

name: De Meern 1
description: Cargo oak vessel, Roman, oared/sailed.  Dating based on ceramics; Jansma, Vorst & Visser date to ca. 148 AD based on dendro dating - suggest revising to more accurate date.

name: Deventer 2
description: Cargo sailed vessel, oak dendro dated

name: Dorestad
description: oak; clinker built, many iron nails. Fragments of ship possibly used as revetment? Cargo identification of objects not certain. wicker basket with stones, unworked animal bones.

name: Dunwich Bank
description: survey find. Dating by 14C.  Anglo-Saxon dugout

name: Fotevik 1
description: Dating based on "typology". Vessel was part of a blockade in the  bay. Oared, sailed?, Nordic oak military vessel

name: Galtabäck 1
description: Dating based on 4 dendro dates from plank ranging 1144-1195.

name: Głębia Gotlandzka
description: No info on dating in database. Slavonic working boat, dugout.

name: Gokstad
description: Dating based on dendro. Military oak and pine oared/sailed vessel, used for  (maybe royal) burial.  Riding equipment, a sled and a tent found inside as well as two human skeletons.

name: Graveney
description: Dating based on 14C (944 ±30) and oak dendro (927±2 and 895±2). Unable to get dendro match in '94.  Cargo vessel in Anglo-Saxon context

name: Haderslev-Møllesstrømmen
description: Survey find. Dendrodated cargo ship, clinker built, probably traded with Ribe where wood may be from.  15-30 tons displacement with maximum cargo of 15-20 tons. Not decorated.

name: Haithabu 1
description: Dating by dendro on (local) oak plank. Military ship. Viking context, "Nordic" type.

name: Herculaneum 2
description: Coordinates from Navis I (40 n 45, 14 e 30 are inaccurate).  Roman context, small boat

name: Immenstaad
description: Dating based on dendro dates obtained on oak. Flat bottomed vessel

name: Ionian 1
description: Survey find. Dating based on anchor shape.

name: Ithaki-Kephalonia
description: Only surveyed by sonar and preliminary dives. At N end of sound between Itaki and Kephalonia.  This wreck has not been assigned a precise identification in this publication.

name: Kamień Pomorski
description: Dating based on 12 dendro samples (mostly from planks) with interval 1076-1150; 3 14C samples (from luting and planks) with interval 1080-1140 and average margin of 45.  Oak, pine cargo vessel, sailed/oared, found in the mouth of the Dziwna river, under 1.2 m of sediment.  Sherds on the layer directly above it were dated 11-12th C.

name: Karschau
description: dendro dated w/15 samples of wood, whose provenance has been ascertained to be from N. Germany and Denmark. Clinker built.

name: Klåstad
description: Dating based on dendro on oak plank. Nordic sailed/oared cargo vessel of beech, oak and pine; in Viking context.

name: Ląd
description: Dating based on 2 14C samples dating 900 and 970 with a margin of 90 yrs each. Dendro dating based on 7 samples of planks gave range 891-1115.  Dendro showed also origin around area of Wroclaw.  Vessel is ash and oak, cargo, Slavonic.

name: Ladby, Kerteminde
description: Dating by 14C on caulking and objects found. Military boat is made of oak. Viking context, "Nordic" type ship.

name: Lone Mushroom
description: Sherds of pottery are similar to the ones of the Na'ama wreck.  Author does not venture to propose a date.

name: Lough Lene
description: Dating of  oak tenon produces a date which according to author may result in early 1st C AD.   Fishing vessel, Mediterranean tradition construction.  Found on lake bed, no depth specified on db, but is 4-5m from IJNA article (1991).

name: Ma'Agan Michael
description: Dating based on shape of ingots (compared to other finds in the Mediterranean, and inscriptions in Greek and Latin engraved on them, possibly indicating  workshop origin.

name: Magor Pill 2
description: Survey find. Dating based on oak dendrodata. Information here provided by a review of nautical archaeology work in UK in 1996.

name: Marseille 5
description: Found in place Jules Verne 4. Roman context.

name: Marseille 8
description: amphora (under hull, type Dressel 1A) is terminus post quem. No precise petrological analysis for whole cargo available yet, but the most valuable colored marble is from Eastern quarries.

name: Mercury wreck
description: Dating based on 14C on planks and ceramic evidence. Utensils found at 28 m depth, deeper than rest of wreck. Anchors present engraved Arabic inscriptions.

name: Na'ama
description: Dating based on 14C and anchor type.  Few remnants of the hull left.

name: Nydam
description: Dating by several dendro samples on oak planks.  Environment is a peat bog.  2 other ships found with it but not cataloged by Navis I.  The description by Otto Uldum says that it has been dated by dendro to 310-320; this conflicts with the Navis I database recorded dates. "Nordic" type, sail/oar.

name: Olbia 1
description: survey find. No dating info provided, except claim that mast found is late Roman and so large that ship would have been 30m in length.  Also mentions in passing that 9+ medieval ships were found in the area but neglected in favor of the single mast.

name: Orlamonde 1
description: between port of Nice and Villefranche

name: Orunia 1
description: No dating info provided. Military vessel, oared, oak pine, Slavonic. Similarity of measurements with Lad boat are suspect.

name: Oseberg
description: Dating based on dendro on plank and gravechamber.  Used as boat burial.  Military Nordic vessel in Viking context.

name: Parco di Teodorico
description: well preserved hull; 200m N of Theoderic's mausoleum

name: Pisa A
description: last voyage was probably down the Adriatic to Africa and up the coast of Italy

name: Plage d'Arles 4
description: From Betica; vestiges of ship wood; dating from Dr20 amphoras; location: several km from Farama

name: Poel (Timmendorfer Mole) 1
description: dendro dated with 30 different samples of pine. 150m north of Timmendorf strand. Could hold up to 200 tons of cargo

name: Porto nuovo (Bonifacio, Corsica)
description: variety of marble indicates Rome as port of origin

name: Puck 2
description: Dating based on 9 dendro samples on oak planks, range 861-978, and 3 14C samples range 555-810, with average margin of 68 years (dismissed by excavator as well as other literature).  Slavonic, military vessel, oared/sailed, oak, pine.  Found in underwater quay, in Gdansk bay, along with another 5 wrecks.  Mast step of wreck 2 preserved very well.

name: Ralswiek 1
description: No info on dating or cargo in Navis database. Slavonic context

name: Ria de Aveiro A
description: Dating based on 4 14C samples on plank, oar, walnut and keg stave. All very reliable, with 2 sigma interval 1424-1469 and intersection in 1441. Ceramics are in perfect condition for the most part, suggesting non violent sinking.

name: River Hamble
description: Archaeologists agree that this is the wreck of the Grâce Dieu, of Henry V.  Dating based on C14 and construction features.

name: Roskilde 1
description: Survey find. Dating by dendro of planks. Cargo vessel in oak and pine. "Nordic" type. Cargo vessel. All Roskilde ships found during foundation work for the Viking ship museum.

name: S. Marco in Boccalama A
description: Derelict, not shipwreck. Rascona, a cargo ship. Dating based on 14C and dendro (sample origin/quality not specified). The dendrodata is not reliable.  There is evidence of a boat storage facility on site in 1328 from archival documents.

name: Saint-Florent 1?
description: not yet defined as a wreck

name: Saintes-Maries 1
description: facing Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer; date from amphoras: C1, perhaps Julio-Claudian or Flavian, perhaps Vespasian

name: San Vito Lo Capo
description: Dating based on artefacts, grooved amphoras (height 42-53 cm) narrow neck and almond lip. Three amphoras found stoppered by a cork.   Norman ship.  Discovered in the 1990s.

name: Skuldelev 1
description: Dating by dendro, 3 samples from planks of oak and pine. Coordinates (55º 39'N 12º 05'E from Navis I db) inaccurate.  Viking context, "Nordic" type.

name: Saint Peter port (Guernsey) 2
description: clinker built, still has keel, floor and keelson.

name: Sutton Hoo
description: Dating based on coin and context.  Ship is Anglo-Saxon and is situated in a graveyard, contains a burial chamber attributed to East Anglian King Raedwald.

name: Szczecin
description: Dating based on 15 dendro dates on oak plank, ranging 787-891.  4 early 14C dates show range 510-670 with average 65 years margin.   One later 14C sample from luting moss showed 840 with a margin of 70 years.  Slavonic, oak, pine, working boat.

name: Tantura lagoon A (Dor J)
description: 25% hull of small coaster; 14C dates wreck to C6; ballast, keel, post, stakes, frames, nail and bolt attachments, caulking in seams. 7 other shipwrecks, one Roman, another a ?galley, not precisely dated are in the same area. 14C dated according to BAR publication

name: Tune
description: Dating based on dendro. Military oared/sailed Nordic oak and pine ship. Later used as a burial ship.  Found in a mound on a farm.

name: Utrecht 1
description: Dating based on oak dendro dates.  Cargo, large sailed vessel.

name: Wismar-Wendorf
description: dendro dated; wood from area around Riga.

name: Woerden 2
description: Roman river boat similar to Woerden 1, oak sailed vessel in Roman context. No dating in db.

name: W-Sinop A
description: Survey. No dating beyond amphora types.

name: Xanten Lower Rhine
description: Survey. "Carolingian riverboat," published in survey news along with the other Xanten boats.  The Carolingian boat was mentioned in passing.

name: Marsaskala
description: Homogenous deposit in the port, possibly wrecked cargo or port dump (i.e. not a wreck)

name: Küçük Ada
description: An associated amphora could indicate C8: Günsenin 2002, 129

name: Krefeld Gellep 1
description: website picture shows it with C13-14 date

name: Port-la-Nautique
description: hull fragments in silted up harbor that was active C1 BC-C1 AD

name: Alexandria late Roman
description: brief mention; covered with coral; <20 m; cargo of LRA1

name: Sudak 1
description: Amphoras have grafitti; anchor type is early: Kapitän D, hence they date wreck C9

name: Sudak 2
description: Dated from amphoras and anchor Kapitän E of same date range.

name: Sudak 3
description: Wreck discovered by fishing trawler, an amphora with Greek inscriptions, photo; localized only to "Sudak Bay"
